title: HiPER: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning with Explicit Credit Assignment for Large Language Model Agents
summary:
Training LLMs as interactive agents for multi-turn decision-making remains challenging, particularly in long-horizon tasks with sparse and delayed rewards, where agents must execute extended sequences of actions before receiving meaningful feedback. Most existing reinforcement learning (RL) approaches model LLM agents as flat policies operating at a single time scale, selecting one action at each turn. In sparse-reward settings, such flat policies must propagate credit across the entire trajectory without explicit temporal abstraction, which often leads to unstable optimization and inefficient credit assignment.
  We propose HiPER, a novel Hierarchical Plan-Execute RL framework that explicitly separates high-level planning from low-level execution. HiPER factorizes the policy into a high-level planner that proposes subgoals and a low-level executor that carries them out over multiple action steps. To align optimization with this structure, we introduce a key technique called hierarchical advantage estimation (HAE), which carefully assigns credit at both the planning and execution levels. By aggregating returns over the execution of each subgoal and coordinating updates across the two levels, HAE provides an unbiased gradient estimator and provably reduces variance compared to flat generalized advantage estimation.
  Empirically, HiPER achieves state-of-the-art performance on challenging interactive benchmarks, reaching 97.4\% success on ALFWorld and 83.3\% on WebShop with Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (+6.6\% and +8.3\% over the best prior method), with especially large gains on long-horizon tasks requiring multiple dependent subtasks. These results highlight the importance of explicit hierarchical decomposition for scalable RL training of multi-turn LLM agents.
tldr: The paper introduces HiPER, a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for LLM agents that separates planning and execution with hierarchical advantage estimation, achieving state-of-the-art performance on long-horizon interactive tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为HiPER的分层强化学习框架, 用于训练LLM智能体。该框架分离了规划和执行, 并采用分层优势估计, 在长时程交互任务中实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh:
将大型语言模型（LLM）训练成交互式智能体以进行多轮决策仍然充满挑战，尤其是在具有稀疏和延迟奖励的长期任务中，智能体必须执行 extended action sequences 才能收到有意义的反馈。大多数现有的强化学习（RL）方法将 LLM 智能体建模为在单一时间尺度上运行的扁平策略，在每个回合选择一个动作。在稀疏奖励设置中，这种扁平策略必须在没有明确时间抽象的情况下传播整个trajectory 的信用，这通常会导致不稳定的优化和低效的信用分配。

我们提出 HiPER，一种新颖的分层计划-执行 RL 框架，它显式地将高层规划与低层执行分离。HiPER 将策略分解为提出子目标的高层规划器和在多个动作步骤中执行这些子目标的低层执行器。为了使优化与这种结构对齐，我们引入了一种名为分层优势估计 (HAE) 的关键技术，该技术仔细地在规划和执行级别分配信用。通过聚合每个子目标执行过程中的回报，并协调两个级别的更新，HAE 提供了一个无偏梯度估计器，并且与扁平广义优势估计相比，可以证明降低了方差。

在实验上，HiPER 在具有挑战性的交互式基准测试中取得了最先进的性能，在使用 Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct 时，在 ALFWorld 上达到 97.4% 的成功率，在 WebShop 上达到 83.3% (+6.6% 和 +8.3%，优于最佳的现有方法)，尤其是在需要多个相关子任务的长期任务中取得了巨大的提升。这些结果突显了显式的分层分解对于多轮 LLM 智能体的可扩展 RL 训练的重要性。

title: VIGOR: Visual Goal-In-Context Inference for Unified Humanoid Fall Safety
summary: Reliable fall recovery is critical for humanoids operating in cluttered environments. Unlike quadrupeds or wheeled robots, humanoids experience high-energy impacts, complex whole-body contact, and large viewpoint changes during a fall, making recovery essential for continued operation. Existing methods fragment fall safety into separate problems such as fall avoidance, impact mitigation, and stand-up recovery, or rely on end-to-end policies trained without vision through reinforcement learning or imitation learning, often on flat terrain. At a deeper level, fall safety is treated as monolithic data complexity, coupling pose, dynamics, and terrain and requiring exhaustive coverage, limiting scalability and generalization. We present a unified fall safety approach that spans all phases of fall recovery. It builds on two insights: 1) Natural human fall and recovery poses are highly constrained and transferable from flat to complex terrain through alignment, and 2) Fast whole-body reactions require integrated perceptual-motor representations. We train a privileged teacher using sparse human demonstrations on flat terrain and simulated complex terrains, and distill it into a deployable student that relies only on egocentric depth and proprioception. The student learns how to react by matching the teacher's goal-in-context latent representation, which combines the next target pose with the local terrain, rather than separately encoding what it must perceive and how it must act. Results in simulation and on a real Unitree G1 humanoid demonstrate robust, zero-shot fall safety across diverse non-flat environments without real-world fine-tuning. The project page is available at https://vigor2026.github.io/
tldr: This paper introduces VIGOR, a vision-guided unified fall safety approach for humanoids that leverages imitation learning from a privileged teacher to a deployable student, demonstrating robust zero-shot transfer to complex terrains.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了VIGOR，一种视觉引导的统一人形机器人跌倒安全方法，该方法利用从特权教师到可部署学生的模仿学习，展示了对复杂地形的强大零样本迁移。
summary_zh:
摘要：
可靠的跌倒恢复对于在杂乱环境中运行的类人机器人至关重要。与四足或轮式机器人不同，类人机器人在跌倒过程中会经历高能量冲击、复杂的全身接触和大幅度的视角变化，因此恢复对于持续运行至关重要。现有方法将跌倒安全分解为独立的子问题，如跌倒避免、冲击缓解和站立恢复，或者依赖于端到端策略，通过不使用视觉的强化学习或模仿学习进行训练，通常是在平坦地形上。更深层次地，跌倒安全被视为单一的数据复杂性，将姿态、动力学和地形耦合在一起，需要详尽的覆盖，限制了可扩展性和泛化能力。我们提出了一种统一的跌倒安全方法，涵盖了跌倒恢复的所有阶段。它基于两个洞见：1) 自然的人类跌倒和恢复姿势是高度约束的，并且可以通过对齐从平坦地形转移到复杂地形，以及 2) 快速的全身反应需要集成的感知-运动表征。我们使用稀疏的人类演示在平坦地形和模拟的复杂地形上训练了一个特权教师，并将其提炼成一个可部署的学生，该学生仅依赖于自我中心的深度信息和本体感受。学生通过匹配教师的上下文目标潜在表征来学习如何反应，该表征将下一个目标姿势与局部地形相结合，而不是分别编码它必须感知什么以及它必须如何行动。在模拟和真实的宇树G1类人机器人上的结果表明，无需真实世界微调的情况下，可以在各种非平坦环境中实现稳健的、零样本的跌倒安全。项目主页可在https://vigor2026.github.io/上找到。

title: RoboGene: Boosting VLA Pre-training via Diversity-Driven Agentic Framework for Real-World Task Generation
summary: The pursuit of general-purpose robotic manipulation is hindered by the scarcity of diverse, real-world interaction data. Unlike data collection from web in vision or language, robotic data collection is an active process incurring prohibitive physical costs. Consequently, automated task curation to maximize data value remains a critical yet under-explored challenge. Existing manual methods are unscalable and biased toward common tasks, while off-the-shelf foundation models often hallucinate physically infeasible instructions. To address this, we introduce RoboGene, an agentic framework designed to automate the generation of diverse, physically plausible manipulation tasks across single-arm, dual-arm, and mobile robots. RoboGene integrates three core components: diversity-driven sampling for broad task coverage, self-reflection mechanisms to enforce physical constraints, and human-in-the-loop refinement for continuous improvement. We conduct extensive quantitative analysis and large-scale real-world experiments, collecting datasets of 18k trajectories and introducing novel metrics to assess task quality, feasibility, and diversity. Results demonstrate that RoboGene significantly outperforms state-of-the-art foundation models (e.g., GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro). Furthermore, real-world experiments show that VLA models pre-trained with RoboGene achieve higher success rates and superior generalization, underscoring the importance of high-quality task generation. Our project is available at https://robogene-boost-vla.github.io.
tldr: RoboGene is an agentic framework for automatically generating diverse and physically plausible robotic manipulation tasks to improve VLA pre-training by addressing data scarcity and feasibility issues. It outperforms existing methods, leading to better VLA model performance in real-world tasks.
tldr_zh: RoboGene是一个agentic框架，通过自动生成多样且物理上可信的机器人操作任务来改进VLA预训练，解决了数据稀缺和可行性问题。它优于现有方法，从而提高了VLA模型在现实任务中的性能。
summary_zh: 通用机器人

title: Articulated 3D Scene Graphs for Open-World Mobile Manipulation
summary: Semantics has enabled 3D scene understanding and affordance-driven object interaction. However, robots operating in real-world environments face a critical limitation: they cannot anticipate how objects move. Long-horizon mobile manipulation requires closing the gap between semantics, geometry, and kinematics. In this work, we present MoMa-SG, a novel framework for building semantic-kinematic 3D scene graphs of articulated scenes containing a myriad of interactable objects. Given RGB-D sequences containing multiple object articulations, we temporally segment object interactions and infer object motion using occlusion-robust point tracking. We then lift point trajectories into 3D and estimate articulation models using a novel unified twist estimation formulation that robustly estimates revolute and prismatic joint parameters in a single optimization pass. Next, we associate objects with estimated articulations and detect contained objects by reasoning over parent-child relations at identified opening states. We also introduce the novel Arti4D-Semantic dataset, which uniquely combines hierarchical object semantics including parent-child relation labels with object axis annotations across 62 in-the-wild RGB-D sequences containing 600 object interactions and three distinct observation paradigms. We extensively evaluate the performance of MoMa-SG on two datasets and ablate key design choices of our approach. In addition, real-world experiments on both a quadruped and a mobile manipulator demonstrate that our semantic-kinematic scene graphs enable robust manipulation of articulated objects in everyday home environments. We provide code and data at: https://momasg.cs.uni-freiburg.de.
tldr: The paper introduces MoMa-SG, a framework for building semantic-kinematic 3D scene graphs for articulated objects, enabling robots to understand and manipulate objects in real-world environments. They also present a new dataset, Arti4D-Semantic, for this task.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 MoMa-SG，一个用于构建铰接物体语义-运动学 3D 场景图的框架，使机器人能够在现实环境中理解和操作物体。他们还提出了一个新的数据集 Arti4D-Semantic，用于此任务。
summary_zh: 语义信息已赋能三维场景理解和基于可供性的物体交互。然而，在真实世界环境中运行的机器人面临一个关键限制：它们无法预测物体的运动方式。长程移动操作需要弥合语义、几何和运动学之间的鸿沟。在这项工作中，我们提出了 MoMa-SG，一个用于构建包含大量可交互物体的铰接场景的语义-运动学三维场景图的新颖框架。给定包含多个物体铰接的RGB-D序列，我们对物体交互进行时间分割，并使用抗遮挡点跟踪来推断物体运动。然后，我们将点轨迹提升到3D空间，并使用一种新颖的统一螺旋估计公式来估计铰接模型，该公式通过一次优化过程稳健地估计旋转和移动关节参数。接下来，我们将物体与估计的铰接相关联，并通过推理已识别的打开状态下的父子关系来检测包含的物体。我们还推出了新的 Arti4D-Semantic 数据集，该数据集独特地将包括父子关系标签的分层物体语义与跨越 62 个真实场景RGB-D序列的物体轴注释相结合，这些序列包含 600 个物体交互和三种不同的观察范式。我们广泛评估了 MoMa-SG 在两个数据集上的性能，并消融了我们方法中的关键设计选择。此外，在四足机器人和移动操作器上的真实世界实验表明，我们的语义-运动学场景图能够对日常家庭环境中铰接的物体进行鲁棒的操作。我们在https://momasg.cs.uni-freiburg.de提供代码和数据。

title: Dual-Quadruped Collaborative Transportation in Narrow Environments via Safe Reinforcement Learning
summary: Collaborative transportation, where multiple robots collaboratively transport a payload, has garnered significant attention in recent years. While ensuring safe and high-performance inter-robot collaboration is critical for effective task execution, it is difficult to pursue in narrow environments where the feasible region is extremely limited. To address this challenge, we propose a novel approach for dual-quadruped collaborative transportation via safe reinforcement learning (RL). Specifically, we model the task as a fully cooperative constrained Markov game, where collision avoidance is formulated as constraints. We introduce a cost-advantage decomposition method that enforces the sum of team constraints to remain below an upper bound, thereby guaranteeing task safety within an RL framework. Furthermore, we propose a constraint allocation method that assigns shared constraints to individual robots to maximize the overall task reward, encouraging autonomous task-assignment among robots, thereby improving collaborative task performance. Simulation and real-time experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior performance and a higher success rate in dual-quadruped collaborative transportation compared to existing methods.
tldr: This paper proposes a safe reinforcement learning approach for dual-quadruped collaborative transportation in narrow environments, using constrained Markov games and a novel cost-advantage decomposition method to guarantee safety and improve performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种基于安全强化学习的双足机器人协作运输方法，用于狭窄环境。该方法使用约束马尔可夫博弈和新颖的成本优势分解方法来保证安全并提高性能。
summary_zh: 多机器人协同运输有效载荷的协作运输近年来受到了广泛关注。保证安全且高性能的机器人间协作对于有效执行任务至关重要，但在可行区域极其受限的狭窄环境中实现这一点颇具挑战。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种基于安全强化学习（RL）的双足机器人协同运输的新方法。具体而言，我们将该任务建模为一个完全合作的约束马尔可夫博弈，其中避碰被形式化为约束。我们引入了一种成本优势分解方法，该方法强制团队约束的总和保持在上限以下，从而在强化学习框架内保证任务安全性。此外，我们提出了一种约束分配方法，该方法将共享约束分配给各个机器人，以最大化整体任务奖励，鼓励机器人之间的自主任务分配，从而提高协作任务的性能。仿真和实时实验结果表明，与现有方法相比，所提出的方法在双足机器人协同运输中实现了卓越的性能和更高的成功率。

title: World Model Failure Classification and Anomaly Detection for Autonomous Inspection
summary: Autonomous inspection robots for monitoring industrial sites can reduce costs and risks associated with human-led inspection. However, accurate readings can be challenging due to occlusions, limited viewpoints, or unexpected environmental conditions. We propose a hybrid framework that combines supervised failure classification with anomaly detection, enabling classification of inspection tasks as a success, known failure, or anomaly (i.e., out-of-distribution) case. Our approach uses a world model backbone with compressed video inputs. This policy-agnostic, distribution-free framework determines classifications based on two decision functions set by conformal prediction (CP) thresholds before a human observer does. We evaluate the framework on gauge inspection feeds collected from office and industrial sites and demonstrate real-time deployment on a Boston Dynamics Spot. Experiments show over 90% accuracy in distinguishing between successes, failures, and OOD cases, with classifications occurring earlier than a human observer. These results highlight the potential for robust, anticipatory failure detection in autonomous inspection tasks or as a feedback signal for model training to assess and improve the quality of training data. Project website: https://autoinspection-classification.github.io
tldr: This paper presents a hybrid framework combining supervised failure classification with anomaly detection for autonomous inspection robots, using a world model backbone and conformal prediction to classify inspection outcomes and detect out-of-distribution cases earlier than human observers.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种混合框架，将监督式故障分类与异常检测相结合，用于自主巡检机器人。该框架使用世界模型骨干网络和共形预测来分类巡检结果，并且可以比人类观察员更早地检测到超出分布范围的案例。
summary_zh: 用于监测工业场所的自主巡检机器人可以降低人工巡检相关的成本和风险。然而，由于遮挡、有限的视角或意外的环境条件，获取准确读数可能具有挑战性。我们提出了一种混合框架，它将监督式故障分类与异常检测相结合，从而能够将巡检任务分类为成功、已知故障或异常（即，超出分布）情况。我们的方法使用具有压缩视频输入的场景模型主干。这种策略无关、分布无关的框架基于由保角预测 (CP) 阈值设定的两个决策函数，在人工观察员之前确定分类结果。我们在从办公室和工业场所收集的仪表巡检数据上评估了该框架，并展示了在Boston Dynamics Spot上的实时部署。实验表明，在区分成功、故障和OOD案例方面，准确率超过90%，并且分类发生时间早于人工观察员。这些结果突出了在自主巡检任务中进行鲁棒、预测性故障检测的潜力，或者作为模型训练的反馈信号，以评估和提高训练数据的质量。项目网站：https://autoinspection-classification.github.io

title: ScenicRules: An Autonomous Driving Benchmark with Multi-Objective Specifications and Abstract Scenarios
summary: Developing autonomous driving systems for complex traffic environments requires balancing multiple objectives, such as avoiding collisions, obeying traffic rules, and making efficient progress. In many situations, these objectives cannot be satisfied simultaneously, and explicit priority relations naturally arise. Also, driving rules require context, so it is important to formally model the environment scenarios within which such rules apply. Existing benchmarks for evaluating autonomous vehicles lack such combinations of multi-objective prioritized rules and formal environment models. In this work, we introduce ScenicRules, a benchmark for evaluating autonomous driving systems in stochastic environments under prioritized multi-objective specifications. We first formalize a diverse set of objectives to serve as quantitative evaluation metrics. Next, we design a Hierarchical Rulebook framework that encodes multiple objectives and their priority relations in an interpretable and adaptable manner. We then construct a compact yet representative collection of scenarios spanning diverse driving contexts and near-accident situations, formally modeled in the Scenic language. Experimental results show that our formalized objectives and Hierarchical Rulebooks align well with human driving judgments and that our benchmark effectively exposes agent failures with respect to the prioritized objectives. Our benchmark can be accessed at https://github.com/BerkeleyLearnVerify/ScenicRules/.
tldr: The paper introduces ScenicRules, a new benchmark for autonomous driving that incorporates prioritized multi-objective specifications and formally modeled, diverse driving scenarios using the Scenic language, addressing limitations in existing benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为ScenicRules的自动驾驶新基准，它结合了优先排序的多目标规范，并使用Scenic语言正式建模了多样化的驾驶场景，从而解决了现有基准的局限性。
summary_zh: 针对复杂交通环境开发自动驾驶系统需要平衡多个目标，例如避免碰撞、遵守交通规则以及高效前进。在许多情况下，这些目标无法同时满足，从而自然而然地产生明确的优先级关系。此外，驾驶规则需要上下文，因此正式地对应用这些规则的环境场景进行建模至关重要。现有的自动驾驶车辆评估基准缺乏这种多目标优先级规则和正式环境模型的组合。在这项工作中，我们提出了ScenicRules，这是一个用于在优先级多目标规范下评估随机环境中自动驾驶系统的基准。我们首先形式化了一组不同的目标，作为定量评估指标。接下来，我们设计了一个分层规则手册框架，以一种可解释和可适应的方式编码多个目标及其优先级关系。然后，我们构建了一个紧凑而具有代表性的场景集合，涵盖了各种驾驶环境和近事故情况，并以Scenic语言正式建模。实验结果表明，我们形式化的目标和分层规则手册与人类驾驶判断高度一致，并且我们的基准有效地揭示了智能体在优先级目标方面的失败之处。我们的基准可在https://github.com/BerkeleyLearnVerify/ScenicRules/访问。

title: ODYN: An All-Shifted Non-Interior-Point Method for Quadratic Programming in Robotics and AI
summary: We introduce ODYN, a novel all-shifted primal-dual non-interior-point quadratic programming (QP) solver designed to efficiently handle challenging dense and sparse QPs. ODYN combines all-shifted nonlinear complementarity problem (NCP) functions with proximal method of multipliers to robustly address ill-conditioned and degenerate problems, without requiring linear independence of the constraints. It exhibits strong warm-start performance and is well suited to both general-purpose optimization, and robotics and AI applications, including model-based control, estimation, and kernel-based learning methods. We provide an open-source implementation and benchmark ODYN on the Maros-Mészáros test set, demonstrating state-of-the-art convergence performance in small-to-high-scale problems. The results highlight ODYN's superior warm-starting capabilities, which are critical in sequential and real-time settings common in robotics and AI. These advantages are further demonstrated by deploying ODYN as the backend of an SQP-based predictive control framework (OdynSQP), as the implicitly differentiable optimization layer for deep learning (ODYNLayer), and the optimizer of a contact-dynamics simulation (ODYNSim).
tldr: The paper introduces ODYN, a novel quadratic programming solver showing state-of-the-art performance and strong warm-starting capabilities, which is suitable for robotics and AI applications like model-based control and deep learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的二次规划求解器ODYN，它具有最先进的性能和强大的热启动能力，适用于机器人和人工智能应用，如基于模型的控制和深度学习。
summary_zh: 我们提出 ODYN，一种新型的全位移原始对偶非内点二次规划 (QP) 求解器，旨在高效处理具有挑战性的稠密和稀疏 QP 问题。ODYN 将全位移非线性互补问题 (NCP) 函数与邻近乘子法相结合，以稳健地解决病态和退化问题，而无需约束的线性无关性。它表现出强大的热启动性能，非常适合通用优化以及机器人和人工智能应用，包括基于模型的控制、估计和基于核的学习方法。我们提供了一个开源实现，并在 Maros-Mészáros 测试集上对 ODYN 进行了基准测试，展示了从小规模到大规模问题的最先进的收敛性能。结果突显了 ODYN 卓越的热启动能力，这在机器人和人工智能领域常见的顺序和实时设置中至关重要。通过将 ODYN 部署为基于 SQP 的预测控制框架（OdynSQP）的后端、深度学习的隐式可微优化层（ODYNLayer）以及接触动力学模拟的优化器（ODYNSim）进一步证明了这些优势。

title: Dex4D: Task-Agnostic Point Track Policy for Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation
summary: Learning generalist policies capable of accomplishing a plethora of everyday tasks remains an open challenge in dexterous manipulation. In particular, collecting large-scale manipulation data via real-world teleoperation is expensive and difficult to scale. While learning in simulation provides a feasible alternative, designing multiple task-specific environments and rewards for training is similarly challenging. We propose Dex4D, a framework that instead leverages simulation for learning task-agnostic dexterous skills that can be flexibly recomposed to perform diverse real-world manipulation tasks. Specifically, Dex4D learns a domain-agnostic 3D point track conditioned policy capable of manipulating any object to any desired pose. We train this 'Anypose-to-Anypose' policy in simulation across thousands of objects with diverse pose configurations, covering a broad space of robot-object interactions that can be composed at test time. At deployment, this policy can be zero-shot transferred to real-world tasks without finetuning, simply by prompting it with desired object-centric point tracks extracted from generated videos. During execution, Dex4D uses online point tracking for closed-loop perception and control. Extensive experiments in simulation and on real robots show that our method enables zero-shot deployment for diverse dexterous manipulation tasks and yields consistent improvements over prior baselines. Furthermore, we demonstrate strong generalization to novel objects, scene layouts, backgrounds, and trajectories, highlighting the robustness and scalability of the proposed framework.
tldr: Dex4D learns a task-agnostic policy for dexterous manipulation by training a point track conditioned policy in simulation and transferring it zero-shot to real-world tasks. The method shows strong generalization and robustness across various manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: Dex4D 学习了一种与任务无关的灵巧操作策略，通过在模拟环境中训练一个点跟踪条件策略，并将其零样本迁移到现实世界的任务中。 该方法在各种操作任务中表现出强大的泛化能力和鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 学习能够完成大量日常任务的通用策略，在灵巧操作领域仍然是一个开放的挑战。特别是，通过真实世界的遥操作收集大规模操作数据既昂贵又难以扩展。尽管在模拟环境中学习提供了一种可行的替代方案，但为训练设计多个特定于任务的环境和奖励同样具有挑战性。我们提出了 Dex4D，一个利用模拟环境学习与任务无关的灵巧技能的框架，这些技能可以灵活地重组以执行各种真实世界的操作任务。具体来说，Dex4D 学习一种与领域无关的 3D 点追踪条件策略，该策略能够将任何物体操作到任何期望的姿态。我们在模拟环境中通过数千个具有不同姿态配置的对象训练这个“任意姿态到任意姿态”策略，覆盖了广泛的机器人-物体交互空间，这些交互可以在测试时进行组合。在部署时，这个策略可以零样本迁移到真实世界的任务，而无需微调，只需用从生成的视频中提取的期望的以物体为中心的点轨迹来提示它。在执行过程中，Dex4D 使用在线点追踪来进行闭环感知和控制。在模拟和真实机器人上的大量实验表明，我们的方法能够对各种灵巧操作任务进行零样本部署，并且相对于先前的基线产生了持续的改进。此外，我们展示了对新颖物体、场景布局、背景和轨迹的强大泛化能力，突出了所提出框架的鲁棒性和可扩展性。

title: Perceptive Humanoid Parkour: Chaining Dynamic Human Skills via Motion Matching
summary: While recent advances in humanoid locomotion have achieved stable walking on varied terrains, capturing the agility and adaptivity of highly dynamic human motions remains an open challenge. In particular, agile parkour in complex environments demands not only low-level robustness, but also human-like motion expressiveness, long-horizon skill composition, and perception-driven decision-making. In this paper, we present Perceptive Humanoid Parkour (PHP), a modular framework that enables humanoid robots to autonomously perform long-horizon, vision-based parkour across challenging obstacle courses. Our approach first leverages motion matching, formulated as nearest-neighbor search in a feature space, to compose retargeted atomic human skills into long-horizon kinematic trajectories. This framework enables the flexible composition and smooth transition of complex skill chains while preserving the elegance and fluidity of dynamic human motions. Next, we train motion-tracking reinforcement learning (RL) expert policies for these composed motions, and distill them into a single depth-based, multi-skill student policy, using a combination of DAgger and RL. Crucially, the combination of perception and skill composition enables autonomous, context-aware decision-making: using only onboard depth sensing and a discrete 2D velocity command, the robot selects and executes whether to step over, climb onto, vault or roll off obstacles of varying geometries and heights. We validate our framework with extensive real-world experiments on a Unitree G1 humanoid robot, demonstrating highly dynamic parkour skills such as climbing tall obstacles up to 1.25m (96% robot height), as well as long-horizon multi-obstacle traversal with closed-loop adaptation to real-time obstacle perturbations.
tldr: This paper presents a framework called Perceptive Humanoid Parkour (PHP) that allows a humanoid robot to autonomously perform vision-based parkour by combining motion matching for skill composition and imitation learning from RL expert policies, validated with real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为Perceptive Humanoid Parkour (PHP) 的框架，该框架允许人形机器人在视觉信息的驱动下自主完成跑酷动作。该框架结合了用于技能组合的运动匹配和从强化学习专家策略中学习的模仿学习方法，并通过真实世界的实验进行了验证。
summary_zh: 尽管最近在类人机器人运动方面取得的进展已经实现了在各种地形上的稳定行走，但捕捉高度动态的人类运动的敏捷性和适应性仍然是一个公开的挑战。特别是，在复杂环境中敏捷的跑酷不仅需要底层的鲁棒性，还需要类人的运动表现力、长时程的技能组合以及感知驱动的决策制定。在本文中，我们提出了感知类人机器人跑酷 (PHP)，这是一个模块化框架，使类人机器人能够在具有挑战性的障碍赛道上自主执行长时程、基于视觉的跑酷。我们的方法首先利用运动匹配，将其表述为特征空间中的最近邻搜索，将重定向的原子人类技能组合成长时程的运动学轨迹。该框架能够在灵活组合和流畅过渡复杂技能链的同时，保留动态人类运动的优雅和流畅性。接下来，我们为这些组合的运动训练运动跟踪强化学习 (RL) 专家策略，并使用 DAgger 和 RL 的组合将它们提炼成一个基于深度信息的、多技能的 student 策略。至关重要的是，感知和技能组合的结合实现了自主的、上下文感知的决策制定：仅使用板载深度传感和一个离散的二维速度指令，机器人就可以选择和执行跨越、攀爬、跳跃或滚动等动作，以应对不同几何形状和高度的障碍物。我们使用 Unitree G1 类人机器人进行了广泛的真实世界实验，验证了我们的框架，展示了高度动态的跑酷技能，例如攀爬高达 1.25 米（机器人身高的 96%）的障碍物，以及具有闭环适应实时障碍物扰动的长时程多障碍物穿越。

title: FAST-EQA: Efficient Embodied Question Answering with Global and Local Region Relevancy
summary: Embodied Question Answering (EQA) combines visual scene understanding, goal-directed exploration, spatial and temporal reasoning under partial observability. A central challenge is to confine physical search to question-relevant subspaces while maintaining a compact, actionable memory of observations. Furthermore, for real-world deployment, fast inference time during exploration is crucial. We introduce FAST-EQA, a question-conditioned framework that (i) identifies likely visual targets, (ii) scores global regions of interest to guide navigation, and (iii) employs Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning over visual memory to answer confidently. FAST-EQA maintains a bounded scene memory that stores a fixed-capacity set of region-target hypotheses and updates them online, enabling robust handling of both single and multi-target questions without unbounded growth. To expand coverage efficiently, a global exploration policy treats narrow openings and doors as high-value frontiers, complementing local target seeking with minimal computation. Together, these components focus the agent's attention, improve scene coverage, and improve answer reliability while running substantially faster than prior approaches. On HMEQA and EXPRESS-Bench, FAST-EQA achieves state-of-the-art performance, while performing competitively on OpenEQA and MT-HM3D.
tldr: FAST-EQA is a question-conditioned framework for Embodied Question Answering (EQA) that uses region-target hypotheses and Chain-of-Thought reasoning to achieve state-of-the-art performance with improved speed and bounded memory usage.
tldr_zh: FAST-EQA是一个具身问答(Embodied Question Answering, EQA)的条件框架，它使用区域-目标假设和思维链(Chain-of-Thought)推理，以提高速度和限制内存使用的方式实现最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 具身问答（EQA）结合了视觉场景理解、目标导向的探索、在部分可观测性下的空间和时间推理。一个核心挑战是在将物理搜索限制在与问题相关的子空间内的同时，保持对观测结果的紧凑且可操作的记忆。此外，对于真实世界的部署来说，探索期间的快速推理时间至关重要。我们引入了FAST-EQA，一个问题条件化的框架，它（i）识别可能的视觉目标，（ii）评分全局感兴趣区域以指导导航，以及（iii）采用基于视觉记忆的思维链（CoT）推理，以自信地回答问题。FAST-EQA维护一个有界的场景记忆，该记忆存储了一组固定容量的区域-目标假设并在线更新它们，从而能够稳健地处理单目标和多目标问题，而无需无限增长。为了有效地扩展覆盖范围，全局探索策略将狭窄的开口和门视为高价值的前沿，以最小的计算量补充局部目标搜索。总而言之，这些组件集中了代理的注意力，改善了场景覆盖范围，并提高了答案的可靠性，同时运行速度明显快于先前的方法。在HMEQA和EXPRESS-Bench上，FAST-EQA实现了最先进的性能，同时在OpenEQA和MT-HM3D上表现出竞争优势。

title: EnterpriseBench Corecraft: Training Generalizable Agents on High-Fidelity RL Environments
summary: We show that training AI agents on high-fidelity reinforcement learning environments produces capabilities that generalize beyond the training distribution. We introduce CoreCraft, the first environment in EnterpriseBench, Surge AI's suite of agentic RL environments. CoreCraft is a fully operational enterprise simulation of a customer support organization, comprising over 2,500 entities across 14 entity types with 23 unique tools, designed to measure whether AI agents can perform the multi-step, domain-specific work that real jobs demand. Frontier models such as GPT-5.2 and Claude Opus 4.6 solve fewer than 30% of tasks when all expert-authored rubric criteria must be satisfied. Using this environment, we train GLM 4.6 with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and adaptive clipping. After a single epoch of training, the model improves from 25.37% to 36.76% task pass rate on held-out evaluation tasks. More importantly, these gains transfer to out-of-distribution benchmarks: +4.5% on BFCL Parallel, +7.4% on Tau2-Bench Retail, and +6.8% on Tool Decathlon (Pass@1). We believe three environment properties are consistent with the observed transfer: task-centric world building that optimizes for diverse, challenging tasks; expert-authored rubrics enabling reliable reward computation; and enterprise workflows that reflect realistic professional patterns. Our results suggest that environment quality, diversity, and realism are key factors enabling generalizable agent capabilities.
tldr: The paper introduces CoreCraft, a high-fidelity enterprise simulation environment for training RL agents, and demonstrates that agents trained on it exhibit generalization to out-of-distribution tasks after training with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). The environment emphasizes task diversity, expert rubrics, and realistic workflows.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为CoreCraft的高保真企业模拟环境，用于训练强化学习智能体。研究表明，通过使用Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) 在该环境中训练的智能体，可以泛化到分布外的任务。该环境强调任务多样性、专家评分标准和真实的工作流程。
summary_zh: 我们证明了在高保真强化学习环境中训练人工智能智能体能够产生超出训练分布的泛化能力。我们推出了CoreCraft，这是Surge AI的智能体强化学习环境套件EnterpriseBench中的首个环境。CoreCraft是一个完全可操作的企业模拟环境，模拟客户支持组织，包含超过2500个实体，涉及14种实体类型和23种独特的工具，旨在衡量人工智能智能体是否能够执行实际工作岗位所需的多步骤、领域特定任务。诸如GPT-5.2和Claude Opus 4.6等前沿模型在必须满足所有专家编写的评分标准的情况下，解决的任务数量少于30%。利用此环境，我们使用Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)和自适应剪裁训练GLM 4.6。经过单轮训练后，该模型在保留的评估任务上的任务通过率从25.37%提高到36.76%。更重要的是，这些增益转移到了超出分布的基准测试中：在BFCL Parallel上获得+4.5%的提升，在Tau2-Bench Retail上获得+7.4%的提升，以及在Tool Decathlon (Pass@1)上获得+6.8%的提升。我们认为，以下三种环境属性与观察到的迁移一致：以任务为中心的世界构建，优化多样化和具有挑战性的任务；专家编写的评分标准，能够实现可靠的奖励计算；以及反映真实专业模式的企业工作流程。我们的结果表明，环境质量、多样性和真实性是实现可泛化智能体能力的关键因素。

title: Learning Personalized Agents from Human Feedback
summary: Modern AI agents are powerful but often fail to align with the idiosyncratic, evolving preferences of individual users. Prior approaches typically rely on static datasets, either training implicit preference models on interaction history or encoding user profiles in external memory. However, these approaches struggle with new users and with preferences that change over time. We introduce Personalized Agents from Human Feedback (PAHF), a framework for continual personalization in which agents learn online from live interaction using explicit per-user memory. PAHF operationalizes a three-step loop: (1) seeking pre-action clarification to resolve ambiguity, (2) grounding actions in preferences retrieved from memory, and (3) integrating post-action feedback to update memory when preferences drift. To evaluate this capability, we develop a four-phase protocol and two benchmarks in embodied manipulation and online shopping. These benchmarks quantify an agent's ability to learn initial preferences from scratch and subsequently adapt to persona shifts. Our theoretical analysis and empirical results show that integrating explicit memory with dual feedback channels is critical: PAHF learns substantially faster and consistently outperforms both no-memory and single-channel baselines, reducing initial personalization error and enabling rapid adaptation to preference shifts.
tldr: The paper introduces Personalized Agents from Human Feedback (PAHF), a framework for continual personalization using explicit memory and dual feedback channels, demonstrating improved learning speed and adaptation to preference shifts in embodied manipulation and online shopping tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种基于人类反馈的个性化智能体框架(PAHF)，它利用显式记忆和双重反馈通道进行持续的个性化学习，并在具身操作和在线购物任务中展示了更快的学习速度和对偏好变化的适应性。
summary_zh: 现代人工智能体性能强大，但常常无法与个体用户特有的、不断演变的需求对齐。以往的方法通常依赖于静态数据集，要么基于交互历史训练隐式偏好模型，要么将用户画像编码到外部存储器中。然而，这些方法在新用户和随时间变化的偏好方面表现不佳。我们引入了基于人类反馈的个性化智能体 (PAHF) 框架，用于持续个性化，其中智能体通过实时交互，使用显式的用户专属记忆进行在线学习。PAHF 将个性化过程操作化为一个三步循环：（1）寻求预行动澄清以解决歧义，（2）基于从记忆中检索的偏好来确定行动，以及（3）整合后行动反馈以在偏好发生变化时更新记忆。为了评估这种能力，我们开发了一个四阶段协议，并在具身操作和在线购物中设计了两个基准测试。这些基准量化了智能体从零开始学习初始偏好以及随后适应角色转变的能力。我们的理论分析和实验结果表明，将显式记忆与双反馈通道相结合至关重要：PAHF 的学习速度显著加快，并且始终优于无记忆和单通道基线，从而减少了初始个性化误差，并实现了对偏好转变的快速适应。

title: Understanding vs. Generation: Navigating Optimization Dilemma in Multimodal Models
summary: Current research in multimodal models faces a key challenge where enhancing generative capabilities often comes at the expense of understanding, and vice versa. We analyzed this trade-off and identify the primary cause might be the potential conflict between generation and understanding, which creates a competitive dynamic within the model. To address this, we propose the Reason-Reflect-Refine (R3) framework. This innovative algorithm re-frames the single-step generation task into a multi-step process of "generate-understand-regenerate". By explicitly leveraging the model's understanding capability during generation, we successfully mitigate the optimization dilemma, achieved stronger generation results and improved understanding ability which are related to the generation process. This offers valuable insights for designing next-generation unified multimodal models. Code is available at https://github.com/sen-ye/R3.
tldr: The paper introduces the Reason-Reflect-Refine (R3) framework to address the trade-off between understanding and generation in multimodal models by reformulating generation into a multi-step process that incorporates understanding.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了Reason-Reflect-Refine (R3)框架，通过将生成重构为一个包含理解的多步骤过程，来解决多模态模型中理解和生成之间的权衡。
summary_zh: 多模态模型当前的研究面临一个关键挑战：提升生成能力往往以牺牲理解能力为代价，反之亦然。我们分析了这种权衡，并认为主要原因可能是生成和理解之间的潜在冲突，这在模型内部产生了一种竞争动态。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了Reason-Reflect-Refine (R3)框架。这个创新算法将单步生成任务重构为“生成-理解-再生成”的多步过程。通过在生成过程中显式地利用模型的理解能力，我们成功地缓解了优化困境，获得了更强的生成结果，并提升了与生成过程相关的理解能力。这为设计下一代统一多模态模型提供了有价值的见解。代码可在https://github.com/sen-ye/R3 找到。

title: MMA: Multimodal Memory Agent
summary: Long-horizon multimodal agents depend on external memory; however, similarity-based retrieval often surfaces stale, low-credibility, or conflicting items, which can trigger overconfident errors. We propose Multimodal Memory Agent (MMA), which assigns each retrieved memory item a dynamic reliability score by combining source credibility, temporal decay, and conflict-aware network consensus, and uses this signal to reweight evidence and abstain when support is insufficient. We also introduce MMA-Bench, a programmatically generated benchmark for belief dynamics with controlled speaker reliability and structured text-vision contradictions. Using this framework, we uncover the "Visual Placebo Effect", revealing how RAG-based agents inherit latent visual biases from foundation models. On FEVER, MMA matches baseline accuracy while reducing variance by 35.2% and improving selective utility; on LoCoMo, a safety-oriented configuration improves actionable accuracy and reduces wrong answers; on MMA-Bench, MMA reaches 41.18% Type-B accuracy in Vision mode, while the baseline collapses to 0.0% under the same protocol. Code: https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/MMA.
tldr: The paper introduces MMA, a multimodal memory agent that uses dynamic reliability scores for retrieved memory items, improving performance and reducing errors in long-horizon tasks. They also introduce MMA-Bench and uncover the Visual Placebo Effect, showing biases in RAG-based agents.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种多模态记忆代理MMA，它使用动态可靠性分数来评估检索到的记忆条目，从而改善了长时域任务中的性能并减少了错误。他们还引入了MMA-Bench，并揭示了视觉安慰剂效应，表明RAG代理存在偏见。
summary_zh: 长周期多模态智能体依赖于外部记忆；然而，基于相似性的检索常会提取到过时、低可信度或冲突的项目，这可能引发过度自信的错误。我们提出多模态记忆智能体(MMA)，它通过结合来源可信度、时间衰减和冲突感知网络共识，为每个检索到的记忆项目分配一个动态可靠性评分，并利用该信号来重新加权证据，并在支持不足时选择弃权。我们还引入了MMA-Bench，这是一个程序化生成的基准，用于评估具有可控说话者可靠性和结构化文本-视觉矛盾的信念动态。利用该框架，我们揭示了“视觉安慰剂效应”，揭示了基于RAG的智能体如何继承来自基础模型的潜在视觉偏差。在FEVER上，MMA在匹配基线准确率的同时，将方差降低了35.2%，并提高了选择性效用；在LoCoMo上，一种面向安全的配置提高了可操作准确率并减少了错误答案；在MMA-Bench上，MMA在视觉模式下达到了41.18%的Type-B准确率，而基线协议在相同协议下崩溃至0.0%。代码：https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/MMA。

title: ReMoRa: Multimodal Large Language Model based on Refined Motion Representation for Long-Video Understanding
summary: While multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown remarkable success across a wide range of tasks, long-form video understanding remains a significant challenge. In this study, we focus on video understanding by MLLMs. This task is challenging because processing a full stream of RGB frames is computationally intractable and highly redundant, as self-attention have quadratic complexity with sequence length. In this paper, we propose ReMoRa, a video MLLM that processes videos by operating directly on their compressed representations. A sparse set of RGB keyframes is retained for appearance, while temporal dynamics are encoded as a motion representation, removing the need for sequential RGB frames. These motion representations act as a compact proxy for optical flow, capturing temporal dynamics without full frame decoding. To refine the noise and low fidelity of block-based motions, we introduce a module to denoise and generate a fine-grained motion representation. Furthermore, our model compresses these features in a way that scales linearly with sequence length. We demonstrate the effectiveness of ReMoRa through extensive experiments across a comprehensive suite of long-video understanding benchmarks. ReMoRa outperformed baseline methods on multiple challenging benchmarks, including LongVideoBench, NExT-QA, and MLVU.
tldr: ReMoRa is a video MLLM that addresses long-video understanding by using refined motion representations for compact and efficient processing, achieving superior performance on long-video benchmarks.
tldr_zh: ReMoRa 是一种视频多模态大语言模型，通过使用精细化的运动表征进行紧凑和高效的处理，解决了长视频理解的难题，并在长视频基准测试上表现出色。
summary_zh: 多模态大型语言模型(MLLMs)在广泛的任务中表现出卓越的成功，然而，长视频理解仍然是一个重要的挑战。本研究聚焦于MLLMs的视频理解能力。该任务具有挑战性，因为处理完整的RGB帧流在计算上是难以实现的，并且高度冗余，因为自注意力机制的计算复杂度与序列长度呈二次方关系。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为ReMoRa的视频MLLM，它通过直接在其压缩表示上操作来处理视频。保留稀疏的RGB关键帧以获取外观信息，而时间动态则被编码为运动表示，从而无需连续的RGB帧。这些运动表示充当光流的紧凑代理，捕获时间动态而无需完全帧解码。为了细化基于块的运动中存在的噪声和低保真度，我们引入了一个模块来降噪并生成精细的运动表示。此外，我们的模型以一种与序列长度呈线性关系的方式压缩这些特征。我们通过在一套全面的长视频理解基准测试中进行的大量实验，证明了ReMoRa的有效性。ReMoRa在多个具有挑战性的基准测试中，包括LongVideoBench、NExT-QA和MLVU，优于基线方法。

title: Factored Latent Action World Models
summary: Learning latent actions from action-free video has emerged as a powerful paradigm for scaling up controllable world model learning. Latent actions provide a natural interface for users to iteratively generate and manipulate videos. However, most existing approaches rely on monolithic inverse and forward dynamics models that learn a single latent action to control the entire scene, and therefore struggle in complex environments where multiple entities act simultaneously. This paper introduces Factored Latent Action Model (FLAM), a factored dynamics framework that decomposes the scene into independent factors, each inferring its own latent action and predicting its own next-step factor value. This factorized structure enables more accurate modeling of complex multi-entity dynamics and improves video generation quality in action-free video settings compared to monolithic models. Based on experiments on both simulation and real-world multi-entity datasets, we find that FLAM outperforms prior work in prediction accuracy and representation quality, and facilitates downstream policy learning, demonstrating the benefits of factorized latent action models.
tldr: The paper introduces Factored Latent Action Model (FLAM), a novel world model that decomposes scenes into independent factors, each with its own latent action, improving prediction accuracy and enabling better video generation and downstream policy learning in complex multi-entity environments.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种新的世界模型，即分解潜在动作模型 (FLAM)，它将场景分解为独立的因素，每个因素都有其自身的潜在动作，从而提高了预测准确性，并在复杂的多实体环境中实现了更好的视频生成和下游策略学习。
summary_zh: 从无动作视频中学习隐式动作已经成为一种强大的范例，可以扩展可控世界模型的学习。隐式动作为用户提供了一个自然的界面，以便迭代地生成和处理视频。然而，大多数现有方法依赖于单一的逆向和正向动力学模型，这些模型学习单个隐式动作来控制整个场景，因此在多个实体同时作用的复杂环境中表现不佳。本文介绍了一种分解隐式动作模型（FLAM），它是一种分解的动力学框架，将场景分解为独立的因素，每个因素推断其自身的隐式动作，并预测其自身的下一步因素值。这种分解的结构能够更准确地建模复杂的多实体动力学，并提高在无动作视频设置中生成视频的质量，优于单一模型。基于对模拟和真实世界多实体数据集的实验，我们发现FLAM在预测精度和表示质量方面优于现有方法，并有助于下游策略学习，证明了分解隐式动作模型的优势。

title: Training-Free Adaptation of Diffusion Models via Doob's $h$-Transform
summary: Adaptation methods have been a workhorse for unlocking the transformative power of pre-trained diffusion models in diverse applications. Existing approaches often abstract adaptation objectives as a reward function and steer diffusion models to generate high-reward samples. However, these approaches can incur high computational overhead due to additional training, or rely on stringent assumptions on the reward such as differentiability. Moreover, despite their empirical success, theoretical justification and guarantees are seldom established. In this paper, we propose DOIT (Doob-Oriented Inference-time Transformation), a training-free and computationally efficient adaptation method that applies to generic, non-differentiable rewards. The key framework underlying our method is a measure transport formulation that seeks to transport the pre-trained generative distribution to a high-reward target distribution. We leverage Doob's $h$-transform to realize this transport, which induces a dynamic correction to the diffusion sampling process and enables efficient simulation-based computation without modifying the pre-trained model. Theoretically, we establish a high probability convergence guarantee to the target high-reward distribution via characterizing the approximation error in the dynamic Doob's correction. Empirically, on D4RL offline RL benchmarks, our method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines while preserving sampling efficiency.
tldr: This paper introduces DOIT, a training-free adaptation method for diffusion models that leverages Doob's h-transform to steer generation towards high-reward samples, achieving state-of-the-art performance on D4RL offline RL benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 这篇论文介绍了DOIT，一种无需训练的扩散模型自适应方法，它利用Doob的h变换来引导生成高奖励样本，并在D4RL离线强化学习基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 适应方法一直是解锁预训练扩散模型在各种应用中变革性力量的主力军。现有的方法通常将适应目标抽象成一个奖励函数，并引导扩散模型生成高奖励样本。然而，这些方法可能由于额外的训练而产生很高的计算开销，或者依赖于对奖励的严格假设，例如可微性。此外，尽管它们在实践中取得了成功，但理论上的论证和保证却很少被建立。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为DOIT（面向Doob的推理时变换）的免训练且计算高效的适应方法，该方法适用于通用的不可微奖励。我们方法背后的关键框架是一种测度传输公式，旨在将预训练的生成分布传输到高奖励目标分布。我们利用Doob的h-变换来实现这种传输，这导致了对扩散采样过程的动态校正，并实现了高效的基于仿真的计算，而无需修改预训练模型。从理论上讲，我们通过刻画动态Doob校正中的近似误差，建立了到目标高奖励分布的高概率收敛保证。在实验上，在D4RL离线强化学习基准测试中，我们的方法始终优于最先进的基线，同时保持了采样效率。

title: Reactive Motion Generation With Particle-Based Perception in Dynamic Environments
summary: Reactive motion generation in dynamic and unstructured scenarios is typically subject to essentially static perception and system dynamics. Reliably modeling dynamic obstacles and optimizing collision-free trajectories under perceptive and control uncertainty are challenging. This article focuses on revealing tight connection between reactive planning and dynamic mapping for manipulators from a model-based perspective. To enable efficient particle-based perception with expressively dynamic property, we present a tensorized particle weight update scheme that explicitly maintains obstacle velocities and covariance meanwhile. Building upon this dynamic representation, we propose an obstacle-aware MPPI-based planning formulation that jointly propagates robot-obstacle dynamics, allowing future system motion to be predicted and evaluated under uncertainty. The model predictive method is shown to significantly improve safety and reactivity with dynamic surroundings. By applying our complete framework in simulated and noisy real-world environments, we demonstrate that explicit modeling of robot-obstacle dynamics consistently enhances performance over state-of-the-art MPPI-based perception-planning baselines avoiding multiple static and dynamic obstacles.
tldr: This paper presents a reactive motion generation framework for manipulators in dynamic environments using particle-based perception with obstacle velocity and covariance estimation, demonstrating improved safety and reactivity compared to MPPI-based baselines.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一个反应式运动生成框架，用于在动态环境中使用基于粒子的感知来控制机械臂。该框架估计障碍物的速度和协方差，与基于MPPI的基线相比，展现了更高的安全性和反应性。
summary_zh: 在动态和非结构化场景中生成反应式运动通常受到静态感知和系统动力学的限制。在感知和控制不确定性的情况下，可靠地建模动态障碍物并优化无碰撞轨迹具有挑战性。本文重点从基于模型的角度揭示机械臂反应式规划和动态地图构建之间的紧密联系。为了实现高效的、具有明确动态属性的基于粒子的感知，我们提出了一种张量化的粒子权重更新方案，该方案显式地维护障碍物的速度和协方差。在此动态表示的基础上，我们提出了一种基于障碍物感知的MPPI规划公式，该公式联合传播机器人-障碍物动力学，从而允许在不确定性下预测和评估未来的系统运动。该模型预测方法被证明可以显著提高动态环境中的安全性和反应性。通过在模拟的和带有噪声的真实环境中应用我们的完整框架，我们证明了对机器人-障碍物动力学的显式建模始终优于避免多个静态和动态障碍物的最先进的基于MPPI的感知-规划基线。

title: Markerless 6D Pose Estimation and Position-Based Visual Servoing for Endoscopic Continuum Manipulators
summary: Continuum manipulators in flexible endoscopic surgical systems offer high dexterity for minimally invasive procedures; however, accurate pose estimation and closed-loop control remain challenging due to hysteresis, compliance, and limited distal sensing. Vision-based approaches reduce hardware complexity but are often constrained by limited geometric observability and high computational overhead, restricting real-time closed-loop applicability. This paper presents a unified framework for markerless stereo 6D pose estimation and position-based visual servoing of continuum manipulators. A photo-realistic simulation pipeline enables large-scale automatic training with pixel-accurate annotations. A stereo-aware multi-feature fusion network jointly exploits segmentation masks, keypoints, heatmaps, and bounding boxes to enhance geometric observability. To enforce geometric consistency without iterative optimization, a feed-forward rendering-based refinement module predicts residual pose corrections in a single pass. A self-supervised sim-to-real adaptation strategy further improves real-world performance using unlabeled data. Extensive real-world validation achieves a mean translation error of 0.83 mm and a mean rotation error of 2.76° across 1,000 samples. Markerless closed-loop visual servoing driven by the estimated pose attains accurate trajectory tracking with a mean translation error of 2.07 mm and a mean rotation error of 7.41°, corresponding to 85% and 59% reductions compared to open-loop control, together with high repeatability in repeated point-reaching tasks. To the best of our knowledge, this work presents the first fully markerless pose-estimation-driven position-based visual servoing framework for continuum manipulators, enabling precise closed-loop control without physical markers or embedded sensing.
tldr: This paper introduces a markerless stereo 6D pose estimation and position-based visual servoing framework for continuum manipulators, using a multi-feature fusion network and sim-to-real adaptation, showing significant improvements compared to open-loop control in real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于连续体机械臂的无标记立体6D姿态估计和基于位置的视觉伺服框架，该框架采用多特征融合网络和从仿真到真实的自适应方法，并在真实世界的实验中显示出比开环控制的显著改进。
summary_zh: 柔性内窥镜手术系统中的连续体机械臂为微创手术提供了高度的灵巧性；然而，由于存在滞后、柔顺性和有限的远端传感能力，精确的姿态估计和闭环控制仍然具有挑战性。基于视觉的方法降低了硬件复杂性，但常常受到几何可观测性有限和高计算开销的限制，从而限制了实时闭环的应用。本文提出了一个统一的框架，用于连续体机械臂的无标记立体6D姿态估计和基于位置的视觉伺服。一个逼真的仿真管道能够利用像素级精确的标注进行大规模自动训练。一个立体感知的多特征融合网络联合利用分割掩码、关键点、热图和边界框来增强几何可观测性。为了在不进行迭代优化的情况下强化几何一致性，一个基于前馈渲染的细化模块只需一次前向传递即可预测残差姿态校正。一种自监督的仿真到真实自适应策略利用未标记的数据进一步提高了真实世界的性能。广泛的真实世界验证在1,000个样本中实现了0.83毫米的平均平移误差和2.76°的平均旋转误差。由估计姿态驱动的无标记闭环视觉伺服实现了精确的轨迹跟踪，平均平移误差为2.07毫米，平均旋转误差为7.41°，与开环控制相比，分别降低了85%和59%，并在重复的点触任务中具有很高的重复性。据我们所知，这项工作提出了第一个完全无标记的、姿态估计驱动的、基于位置的连续体机械臂视觉伺服框架，无需物理标记或嵌入式传感即可实现精确的闭环控制。

title: Markerless Robot Detection and 6D Pose Estimation for Multi-Agent SLAM
summary: The capability of multi-robot SLAM approaches to merge localization history and maps from different observers is often challenged by the difficulty in establishing data association. Loop closure detection between perceptual inputs of different robotic agents is easily compromised in the context of perceptual aliasing, or when perspectives differ significantly. For this reason, direct mutual observation among robots is a powerful way to connect partial SLAM graphs, but often relies on the presence of calibrated arrays of fiducial markers (e.g., AprilTag arrays), which severely limits the range of observations and frequently fails under sharp lighting conditions, e.g., reflections or overexposure. In this work, we propose a novel solution to this problem leveraging recent advances in Deep-Learning-based 6D pose estimation. We feature markerless pose estimation as part of a decentralized multi-robot SLAM system and demonstrate the benefit to the relative localization accuracy among the robotic team. The solution is validated experimentally on data recorded in a test field campaign on a planetary analogous environment.
tldr: This paper presents a markerless robot detection and 6D pose estimation method for multi-agent SLAM, improving relative localization accuracy in environments where traditional marker-based systems fail.
tldr_zh: 这篇论文提出了一种用于多智能体SLAM的无标记机器人检测和6D姿态估计方法，提高了在传统基于标记的系统失效的环境中的相对定位精度。
summary_zh: 多机器人SLAM方法融合来自不同观测者的定位历史和地图的能力，常常受到数据关联建立难度的挑战。在感知混淆或视角差异显著的情况下，不同机器人代理的感知输入之间的回环检测容易受到影响。因此，机器人间的直接相互观测是连接部分SLAM图的一种有效方式，但通常依赖于使用标定过的信标标记阵列（例如，AprilTag阵列），这严重限制了观测范围，并且在强烈的光照条件下（例如，反射或过度曝光）经常失效。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种利用基于深度学习的6D位姿估计最新进展的全新解决方案来解决这个问题。我们将无标记位姿估计作为去中心化多机器人SLAM系统的一部分，并展示了其对机器人团队间相对定位精度的提升。该解决方案已通过在行星模拟环境的试验场活动中记录的数据进行了实验验证。

title: SIT-LMPC: Safe Information-Theoretic Learning Model Predictive Control for Iterative Tasks
summary: Robots executing iterative tasks in complex, uncertain environments require control strategies that balance robustness, safety, and high performance. This paper introduces a safe information-theoretic learning model predictive control (SIT-LMPC) algorithm for iterative tasks. Specifically, we design an iterative control framework based on an information-theoretic model predictive control algorithm to address a constrained infinite-horizon optimal control problem for discrete-time nonlinear stochastic systems. An adaptive penalty method is developed to ensure safety while balancing optimality. Trajectories from previous iterations are utilized to learn a value function using normalizing flows, which enables richer uncertainty modeling compared to Gaussian priors. SIT-LMPC is designed for highly parallel execution on graphics processing units, allowing efficient real-time optimization. Benchmark simulations and hardware experiments demonstrate that SIT-LMPC iteratively improves system performance while robustly satisfying system constraints.
tldr: The paper introduces SIT-LMPC, a safe and efficient learning-based model predictive control algorithm for iterative robot tasks in uncertain environments, using normalizing flows for value function learning and GPU-based parallel optimization to balance performance and safety.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SIT-LMPC，一种安全高效的基于学习的模型预测控制算法，用于不确定环境中迭代机器人任务，它使用归一化流进行值函数学习，并使用基于GPU的并行优化来平衡性能和安全性。
summary_zh: 在复杂、不确定的环境中执行迭代任务的机器人需要能够平衡鲁棒性、安全性和高性能的控制策略。本文介绍了一种用于迭代任务的安全信息论学习模型预测控制（SIT-LMPC）算法。具体而言，我们设计了一个基于信息论模型预测控制算法的迭代控制框架，以解决离散时间非线性随机系统的受约束无限时域最优控制问题。开发了一种自适应惩罚方法，以确保安全性的同时兼顾最优性。利用先前迭代的轨迹，使用归一化流来学习值函数，与高斯先验相比，这能够实现更丰富的非确定性建模。SIT-LMPC 旨在在图形处理单元上进行高度并行执行，从而实现高效的实时优化。基准模拟和硬件实验表明，SIT-LMPC 能够迭代地提高系统性能，同时稳健地满足系统约束。

title: Reactive Slip Control in Multifingered Grasping: Hybrid Tactile Sensing and Internal-Force Optimization
summary: We present a hybrid learning and model-based approach that adapts internal grasp forces to halt in-hand slip on a multifingered robotic gripper. A multimodal tactile stack combines piezoelectric (PzE) sensing for fast slip cues with piezoresistive (PzR) arrays for contact localization, enabling online construction of the grasp matrix. Upon slip, we update internal forces computed in the null space of the grasp via a quadratic program that preserves the object wrench while enforcing actuation limits. The pipeline yields a theoretical sensing-to-command latency of 35-40 ms, with 5 ms for PzR-based contact and geometry updates and about 4 ms for the quadratic program solve. In controlled trials, slip onset is detected at 20ms. We demonstrate closed-loop stabilization on multifingered grasps under external perturbations. Augmenting efficient analytic force control with learned tactile cues yields both robustness and rapid reactions, as confirmed in our end-to-end evaluation. Measured delays are dominated by the experimental data path rather than actual computation. The analysis outlines a clear route to sub-50 ms closed-loop stabilization.
tldr: This paper presents a hybrid tactile sensing and model-based control system for reactive slip control in multifingered robotic grasping, achieving fast and robust stabilization under external perturbations. It combines piezoelectric and piezoresistive sensors with a quadratic programming approach for internal force optimization.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种混合触觉传感和基于模型的控制系统，用于多指机器人抓取中的反应式滑移控制，实现了外部扰动下的快速和稳健的稳定。它结合了压电和压阻传感器以及二次规划方法，用于内部力优化。
summary_zh: 我们提出了一种混合学习和基于模型的方法，该方法能够调整多指机器人夹持器上的内部抓取力，以阻止掌内滑动。多模态触觉堆叠结合了压电 (PzE) 传感，用于快速滑动提示，以及压阻 (PzR) 阵列，用于接触定位，从而能够在线构建抓取矩阵。一旦发生滑动，我们会通过二次规划来更新抓取零空间中计算出的内力，该二次规划在保持物体扳手的同时，强制执行驱动限制。该流程的理论传感-命令延迟为 35-40 毫秒，其中 5 毫秒用于基于 PzR 的接触和几何更新，约 4 毫秒用于求解二次规划。在受控试验中，滑动开始在 20 毫秒时被检测到。 我们证明了在外部扰动下，多指抓取的闭环稳定。 用学习到的触觉提示来增强高效解析力控制，从而产生鲁棒性和快速响应，这在我们端到端的评估中得到了证实。 测得的延迟主要由实验数据路径决定，而不是实际的计算。 该分析概述了一条实现亚 50 毫秒闭环稳定的清晰途径。

title: The human intention. A taxonomy attempt and its applications to robotics
summary: Despite a surge in robotics research dedicated to inferring and understanding human intent, a universally accepted definition remains elusive since existing works often equate human intention with specific task-related goals. This article seeks to address this gap by examining the multifaceted nature of intention. Drawing on insights from psychology, it attempts to consolidate a definition of intention into a comprehensible framework for a broader audience. The article classifies different types of intention based on psychological and communication studies, offering guidance to researchers shifting from pure technical enhancements to a more human-centric perspective in robotics. It then demonstrates how various robotics studies can be aligned with these intention categories. Finally, through in-depth analyses of collaborative search and object transport use cases, the article underscores the significance of considering the diverse facets of human intention.
tldr: This paper proposes a taxonomy of human intentions for robotics, drawing from psychology and communication studies, and demonstrates its application in collaborative robotics scenarios.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于机器人的意图分类法，该分类法借鉴了心理学和传播学研究，并演示了其在协作机器人场景中的应用。
summary_zh: 尽管致力于推断和理解人类意图的机器人技术研究激增，但至今尚未形成普遍接受的定义，因为现有工作通常将人类意图等同于特定的任务相关目标。本文旨在通过考察意图的多方面性质来弥合这一差距。借鉴心理学的见解，本文试图将意图的定义整合为一个更广泛受众可以理解的框架。本文基于心理学和传播学研究，对不同类型的意图进行分类，为研究人员从纯粹的技术增强转向机器人技术中更以人为中心的视角提供指导。然后，本文展示了如何将各种机器人技术研究与这些意图类别对齐。最后，通过对协作搜索和物体运输用例的深入分析，本文强调了考虑人类意图各个方面的重要性。

title: Solving Parameter-Robust Avoid Problems with Unknown Feasibility using Reinforcement Learning
summary: Recent advances in deep reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved strong results on high-dimensional control tasks, but applying RL to reachability problems raises a fundamental mismatch: reachability seeks to maximize the set of states from which a system remains safe indefinitely, while RL optimizes expected returns over a user-specified distribution. This mismatch can result in policies that perform poorly on low-probability states that are still within the safe set. A natural alternative is to frame the problem as a robust optimization over a set of initial conditions that specify the initial state, dynamics and safe set, but whether this problem has a solution depends on the feasibility of the specified set, which is unknown a priori. We propose Feasibility-Guided Exploration (FGE), a method that simultaneously identifies a subset of feasible initial conditions under which a safe policy exists, and learns a policy to solve the reachability problem over this set of initial conditions. Empirical results demonstrate that FGE learns policies with over 50% more coverage than the best existing method for challenging initial conditions across tasks in the MuJoCo simulator and the Kinetix simulator with pixel observations.
tldr: This paper introduces Feasibility-Guided Exploration (FGE), a reinforcement learning method that simultaneously identifies feasible initial conditions and learns a safe policy for reachability problems, demonstrating improved coverage compared to existing methods. They demonstrate it on MuJoCo and Kinetix.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为可行性引导探索 (FGE) 的强化学习方法，该方法同时识别可行的初始条件，并学习解决可达性问题的安全策略，与现有方法相比，覆盖范围有所提高。他们在 MuJoCo 和 Kinetix 上展示了这一点。
summary_zh: 深度强化学习（RL）的最新进展在高维控制任务中取得了显著成果，但将 RL 应用于可达性问题时，会产生一个根本性的不匹配：可达性寻求最大化系统可无限期保持安全的状态集合，而 RL 则优化用户指定分布上的期望回报。这种不匹配可能导致策略在低概率状态下表现不佳，即使这些状态仍在安全集合中。一个自然的替代方案是将问题构建为对一组指定初始状态、动力学和安全集合的初始条件的鲁棒优化，但该问题是否存在解取决于指定集合的可行性，而可行性是先验未知的。我们提出可行性引导探索（FGE），这是一种同时识别可行初始条件子集（在该子集下存在安全策略）并学习策略以解决该初始条件集合上的可达性问题的方法。经验结果表明，在 MuJoCo 模拟器和具有像素观测的 Kinetix 模拟器中的挑战性初始条件下，FGE 学习到的策略比现有最佳方法的可达范围覆盖率高出 50% 以上。

title: Almost Sure Convergence of Differential Temporal Difference Learning for Average Reward Markov Decision Processes
summary: The average reward is a fundamental performance metric in reinforcement learning (RL) focusing on the long-run performance of an agent. Differential temporal difference (TD) learning algorithms are a major advance for average reward RL as they provide an efficient online method to learn the value functions associated with the average reward in both on-policy and off-policy settings. However, existing convergence guarantees require a local clock in learning rates tied to state visit counts, which practitioners do not use and does not extend beyond tabular settings. We address this limitation by proving the almost sure convergence of on-policy $n$-step differential TD for any $n$ using standard diminishing learning rates without a local clock. We then derive three sufficient conditions under which off-policy $n$-step differential TD also converges without a local clock. These results strengthen the theoretical foundations of differential TD and bring its convergence analysis closer to practical implementations.
tldr: This paper proves the almost sure convergence of on-policy and provides convergence conditions for off-policy differential TD learning using standard diminishing learning rates, addressing limitations of prior work that required impractical local clocks.
tldr_zh: 本文证明了使用标准递减学习率的在线策略和提供了离线策略微分TD学习的几乎必然收敛的收敛条件，解决了之前需要不切实际的本地时钟的工作的局限性。
summary_zh: 平均奖励是强化学习中一个重要的性能指标，它关注智能体长期性能。差分时序差分(TD)学习算法是平均奖励强化学习的一个主要进展，因为它们提供了一种高效的在线方法，可以在同策略和异策略设置下学习与平均奖励相关的价值函数。然而，现有的收敛性保证需要在学习率中引入与状态访问计数相关的局部时钟，从业者通常不使用这种方法，且无法推广到表格设置之外。我们通过证明对于任意$n$，采用标准递减学习率且不带局部时钟的同策略$n$步差分TD几乎必然收敛，来解决这个限制。然后，我们推导出三个充分条件，在此条件下，异策略$n$步差分TD也能在不带局部时钟的情况下收敛。这些结果加强了差分TD的理论基础，并使其收敛性分析更接近实际应用。

title: Verifiable Semantics for Agent-to-Agent Communication
summary: Multiagent AI systems require consistent communication, but we lack methods to verify that agents share the same understanding of the terms used. Natural language is interpretable but vulnerable to semantic drift, while learned protocols are efficient but opaque. We propose a certification protocol based on the stimulus-meaning model, where agents are tested on shared observable events and terms are certified if empirical disagreement falls below a statistical threshold. In this protocol, agents restricting their reasoning to certified terms ("core-guarded reasoning") achieve provably bounded disagreement. We also outline mechanisms for detecting drift (recertification) and recovering shared vocabulary (renegotiation). In simulations with varying degrees of semantic divergence, core-guarding reduces disagreement by 72-96%. In a validation with fine-tuned language models, disagreement is reduced by 51%. Our framework provides a first step towards verifiable agent-to-agent communication.
tldr: This paper introduces a certification protocol for agent communication based on shared observable events, aiming to reduce semantic disagreement between agents, with promising simulation and language model results.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于共享可观察事件的 agent 通信认证协议，旨在减少 agent 之间的语义分歧。模拟和语言模型实验结果均表明该方法具有潜力。
summary_zh: 多智能体人工智能系统需要一致的通信，但我们缺乏验证智能体对所用术语理解一致性的方法。自然语言可解释但易受语义漂移影响，而学习到的协议效率高但不透明。我们提出了一种基于刺激-意义模型的认证协议，其中智能体在共享可观察事件上进行测试，如果经验性分歧低于统计阈值，则术语通过认证。在该协议中，将推理限制在认证术语上的智能体（“核心保护推理”）可实现可证明有界的意见分歧。我们还概述了检测漂移（重新认证）和恢复共享词汇表（重新协商）的机制。在具有不同程度语义差异的模拟中，核心保护将意见分歧降低了 72-96%。在使用微调语言模型的验证中，意见分歧降低了 51%。我们的框架为可验证的智能体间通信提供了第一步。

title: Multi-agent cooperation through in-context co-player inference
summary: Achieving cooperation among self-interested agents remains a fundamental challenge in multi-agent reinforcement learning. Recent work showed that mutual cooperation can be induced between "learning-aware" agents that account for and shape the learning dynamics of their co-players. However, existing approaches typically rely on hardcoded, often inconsistent, assumptions about co-player learning rules or enforce a strict separation between "naive learners" updating on fast timescales and "meta-learners" observing these updates. Here, we demonstrate that the in-context learning capabilities of sequence models allow for co-player learning awareness without requiring hardcoded assumptions or explicit timescale separation. We show that training sequence model agents against a diverse distribution of co-players naturally induces in-context best-response strategies, effectively functioning as learning algorithms on the fast intra-episode timescale. We find that the cooperative mechanism identified in prior work-where vulnerability to extortion drives mutual shaping-emerges naturally in this setting: in-context adaptation renders agents vulnerable to extortion, and the resulting mutual pressure to shape the opponent's in-context learning dynamics resolves into the learning of cooperative behavior. Our results suggest that standard decentralized reinforcement learning on sequence models combined with co-player diversity provides a scalable path to learning cooperative behaviors.
tldr: This paper demonstrates that sequence model agents trained against diverse co-players can achieve cooperation through in-context learning, eliminating the need for hardcoded assumptions or explicit timescale separation.
tldr_zh: 本文展示了通过针对多样化对手训练的序列模型智能体，可以通过上下文学习实现合作，从而消除了对硬编码假设或显式时间尺度分离的需求。
summary_zh: 在多智能体强化学习中，实现自利智能体之间的合作仍然是一个根本性的挑战。最近的研究表明，在“学习感知型”智能体之间可以诱导出相互合作，这些智能体能够考虑到并塑造其协同参与者的学习动态。然而，现有方法通常依赖于硬编码且往往前后不一致的关于协同参与者学习规则的假设，或者强制性地将“朴素学习者”（在快速时间尺度上更新）和“元学习者”（观察这些更新）严格区分开来。在这里，我们证明了序列模型的上下文学习能力允许在无需硬编码假设或显式时间尺度分离的情况下实现对协同参与者学习的感知。我们展示了针对多样化协同参与者分布训练序列模型智能体自然地诱导了上下文最优响应策略，从而在快速的幕内时间尺度上有效地充当学习算法。我们发现，先前的研究工作中发现的合作机制——对敲诈的脆弱性驱动了相互塑造——在这种情况下自然而然地出现：上下文适应使得智能体易受敲诈，而由此产生的相互压力促使塑造对手的上下文学习动态，最终转化为合作行为的学习。我们的结果表明，在序列模型上进行标准的分散式强化学习，并结合协同参与者的多样性，为学习合作行为提供了一条可扩展的路径。

title: Graphon Mean-Field Subsampling for Cooperative Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
summary: Coordinating large populations of interacting agents is a central challenge in multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), where the size of the joint state-action space scales exponentially with the number of agents. Mean-field methods alleviate this burden by aggregating agent interactions, but these approaches assume homogeneous interactions. Recent graphon-based frameworks capture heterogeneity, but are computationally expensive as the number of agents grows. Therefore, we introduce $\texttt{GMFS}$, a $\textbf{G}$raphon $\textbf{M}$ean-$\textbf{F}$ield $\textbf{S}$ubsampling framework for scalable cooperative MARL with heterogeneous agent interactions. By subsampling $κ$ agents according to interaction strength, we approximate the graphon-weighted mean-field and learn a policy with sample complexity $\mathrm{poly}(κ)$ and optimality gap $O(1/\sqrtκ)$. We verify our theory with numerical simulations in robotic coordination, showing that $\texttt{GMFS}$ achieves near-optimal performance.
tldr: The paper introduces Graphon Mean-Field Subsampling (GMFS) for scalable cooperative MARL with heterogeneous agent interactions, achieving near-optimal performance by subsampling agents based on interaction strength.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种用于可扩展的具有异构智能体交互的合作多智能体强化学习的 Graphon 均值场子采样（GMFS）方法，通过基于交互强度对智能体进行子采样，实现了接近最优的性能。
summary_zh: 协调大量交互智能体是多智能体强化学习（MARL）中的核心挑战，因为联合状态-动作空间的大小随智能体数量呈指数级增长。均场方法通过聚合智能体交互来缓解这一负担，但这些方法假定交互是同质的。最近基于图论核的框架可以捕获异质性，但随着智能体数量的增长，计算成本很高。因此，我们引入了$\texttt{GMFS}$，一种$\textbf{G}$raphon $\textbf{M}$ean-$\textbf{F}$ield $\textbf{S}$ubsampling（图论核均场子采样）框架，用于解决具有异构智能体交互的可扩展协作MARL问题。通过根据交互强度对$κ$个智能体进行子采样，我们近似图论核加权均场，并学习一个复杂度为$\mathrm{poly}(κ)$且最优性差距为$O(1/\sqrtκ)$的策略。我们在机器人协调的数值模拟中验证了我们的理论，表明$\texttt{GMFS}$实现了接近最优的性能。

title: ReLoop: Structured Modeling and Behavioral Verification for Reliable LLM-Based Optimization
summary: Large language models (LLMs) can translate natural language into optimization code, but silent failures pose a critical risk: code that executes and returns solver-feasible solutions may encode semantically incorrect formulations, creating a feasibility-correctness gap of up to 90 percentage points on compositional problems. We introduce ReLoop, addressing silent failures from two complementary directions. Structured generation decomposes code production into a four-stage reasoning chain (understand, formalize, synthesize, verify) that mirrors expert modeling practice, with explicit variable-type reasoning and self-verification to prevent formulation errors at their source. Behavioral verification detects errors that survive generation by testing whether the formulation responds correctly to solver-based parameter perturbation, without requiring ground truth -- an external semantic signal that bypasses the self-consistency problem inherent in LLM-based code review. The two mechanisms are complementary: structured generation dominates on complex compositional problems, while behavioral verification becomes the largest single contributor on problems with localized formulation defects. Together with execution recovery via IIS-enhanced diagnostics, ReLoop raises correctness from 22.6% to 31.1% and execution from 72.1% to 100.0% on the strongest model, with consistent gains across five models spanning three paradigms (foundation, SFT, RL) and three benchmarks. We additionally release RetailOpt-190, 190 compositional retail optimization scenarios targeting the multi-constraint interactions where LLMs most frequently fail.
tldr: ReLoop introduces a structured generation and behavioral verification approach to improve the correctness and execution rates of LLM-generated optimization code, particularly for compositional problems. They also release a new compositional retail optimization benchmark, RetailOpt-190.
tldr_zh: ReLoop提出了一种结构化生成和行为验证方法，旨在提高LLM生成的优化代码的正确性和执行率，尤其是在组合问题中。他们还发布了一个新的组合零售优化基准测试，RetailOpt-190。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）可以将自然语言翻译成优化代码，但静默失败构成了一个关键风险：执行并返回求解器可行解的代码可能编码了语义不正确的公式，在组合问题上造成高达 90 个百分点的可行性-正确性差距。我们引入了 ReLoop，从两个互补的方向解决静默失败问题。结构化生成将代码生成分解为四个阶段的推理链（理解、形式化、综合、验证），镜像了专家建模实践，通过显式的变量类型推理和自我验证来从源头上防止公式错误。行为验证通过测试公式是否对基于求解器的参数扰动做出正确响应来检测生成过程中遗留的错误，无需真实值——这是一种绕过 LLM 编码审查中固有的自我一致性问题的外部语义信号。这两种机制是互补的：结构化生成在复杂的组合问题上占主导地位，而行为验证则成为具有局部公式缺陷的问题的最大单一贡献者。结合通过 IIS 增强诊断实现的执行恢复，ReLoop 将最强模型的正确率从 22.6% 提高到 31.1%，并将执行率从 72.1% 提高到 100.0%，并在涵盖三种范式（基础模型、SFT 模型、RL 模型）和三个基准测试的五种模型中取得了一致的提升。我们还发布了 RetailOpt-190，这是一个包含 190 个组合零售优化场景的数据集，旨在针对 LLM 最容易失败的多约束交互。

title: From Tool Orchestration to Code Execution: A Study of MCP Design Choices
summary: Model Context Protocols (MCPs) provide a unified platform for agent systems to discover, select, and orchestrate tools across heterogeneous execution environments. As MCP-based systems scale to incorporate larger tool catalogs and multiple concurrently connected MCP servers, traditional tool-by-tool invocation increases coordination overhead, fragments state management, and limits support for wide-context operations. To address these scalability challenges, recent MCP designs have incorporated code execution as a first-class capability, an approach called Code Execution MCP (CE-MCP). This enables agents to consolidate complex workflows, such as SQL querying, file analysis, and multi-step data transformations, into a single program that executes within an isolated runtime environment. In this work, we formalize the architectural distinction between context-coupled (traditional) and context-decoupled (CE-MCP) models, analyzing their fundamental scalability trade-offs. Using the MCP-Bench framework across 10 representative servers, we empirically evaluate task behavior, tool utilization patterns, execution latency, and protocol efficiency as the scale of connected MCP servers and available tools increases, demonstrating that while CE-MCP significantly reduces token usage and execution latency, it introduces a vastly expanded attack surface. We address this security gap by applying the MAESTRO framework, identifying sixteen attack classes across five execution phases-including specific code execution threats such as exception-mediated code injection and unsafe capability synthesis. We validate these vulnerabilities through adversarial scenarios across multiple LLMs and propose a layered defense architecture comprising containerized sandboxing and semantic gating. Our findings provide a rigorous roadmap for balancing scalability and security in production-ready executable agent workflows.
tldr: This paper explores the trade-offs between scalability and security in Model Context Protocols (MCPs), particularly focusing on Code Execution MCPs (CE-MCPs), and proposes a defense architecture against identified vulnerabilities.
tldr_zh: 本文探讨了模型上下文协议（MCP）中可扩展性和安全性之间的权衡，特别是关注代码执行MCP（CE-MCP），并提出了针对已识别漏洞的防御架构。
summary_zh: 模型上下文协议 (MCPs) 提供了一个统一的平台，供智能体系统在异构执行环境中发现、选择和编排工具。随着基于 MCP 的系统扩展以纳入更大的工具目录和多个并发连接的 MCP 服务器，传统的逐工具调用增加了协调开销，碎片化了状态管理，并限制了对广上下文操作的支持。为了解决这些可伸缩性挑战，最近的 MCP 设计已将代码执行作为一种首要能力，这种方法被称为代码执行 MCP (CE-MCP)。这使智能体能够将复杂的工作流程（例如 SQL 查询、文件分析和多步骤数据转换）整合到单个程序中，该程序在隔离的运行时环境中执行。在这项工作中，我们形式化了上下文耦合（传统）模型和上下文解耦（CE-MCP）模型之间的架构区别，分析了它们的基本可伸缩性权衡。我们使用 MCP-Bench 框架，跨 10 个具有代表性的服务器，实证评估了任务行为、工具利用率模式、执行延迟和协议效率，随着连接的 MCP 服务器和可用工具的规模增加，我们证明了 CE-MCP 虽然显著减少了令牌使用量和执行延迟，但也引入了大大扩大的攻击面。我们通过应用 MAESTRO 框架来解决此安全漏洞，识别了跨五个执行阶段的十六个攻击类别——包括特定的代码执行威胁，例如异常介导的代码注入和不安全的能力合成。我们通过跨多个 LLM 的对抗场景验证了这些漏洞，并提出了一种包含容器化沙箱和语义门控的分层防御体系结构。我们的研究结果为平衡生产就绪的可执行智能体工作流程中的可伸缩性和安全性提供了一个严格的路线图。

title: Developing AI Agents with Simulated Data: Why, what, and how?
summary: As insufficient data volume and quality remain the key impediments to the adoption of modern subsymbolic AI, techniques of synthetic data generation are in high demand. Simulation offers an apt, systematic approach to generating diverse synthetic data. This chapter introduces the reader to the key concepts, benefits, and challenges of simulation-based synthetic data generation for AI training purposes, and to a reference framework to describe, design, and analyze digital twin-based AI simulation solutions.
tldr: This paper introduces simulation-based synthetic data generation for training AI agents, focusing on its benefits, challenges, and a reference framework for digital twin-based AI simulation solutions.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种基于模拟的合成数据生成方法，用于训练人工智能代理，重点介绍了其优势、挑战以及基于数字孪生的AI模拟解决方案的参考框架。
summary_zh: 由于数据

title: GlobeDiff: State Diffusion Process for Partial Observability in Multi-Agent Systems
summary: In the realm of multi-agent systems, the challenge of \emph{partial observability} is a critical barrier to effective coordination and decision-making. Existing approaches, such as belief state estimation and inter-agent communication, often fall short. Belief-based methods are limited by their focus on past experiences without fully leveraging global information, while communication methods often lack a robust model to effectively utilize the auxiliary information they provide. To solve this issue, we propose Global State Diffusion Algorithm~(GlobeDiff) to infer the global state based on the local observations. By formulating the state inference process as a multi-modal diffusion process, GlobeDiff overcomes ambiguities in state estimation while simultaneously inferring the global state with high fidelity. We prove that the estimation error of GlobeDiff under both unimodal and multi-modal distributions can be bounded. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that GlobeDiff achieves superior performance and is capable of accurately inferring the global state.
tldr: The paper introduces GlobeDiff, a diffusion-based method for inferring global state from local observations in multi-agent systems, addressing the limitations of belief-based and communication-based approaches in partially observable environments. It claims superior performance in accurately inferring the global state.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了GlobeDiff，一种基于扩散的方法，用于在多智能体系统中从局部观察中推断全局状态，解决了在部分可观察环境中基于信念和基于通信方法局限性。 它声称在准确推断全局状态方面具有卓越的性能。
summary_zh: 在多智能体系统领域，\emph{部分可观测性}的挑战是有效协调和决策的关键障碍。现有的方法，如信念状态估计和智能体间通信，往往有所不足。基于信念的方法受限于其对过去经验的关注，而未能充分利用全局信息，而通信方法通常缺乏一个鲁棒的模型来有效地利用它们提供的辅助信息。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种全局状态扩散算法（GlobeDiff），以基于局部观测来推断全局状态。通过将状态推断过程表示为一个多模态扩散过程，GlobeDiff克服了状态估计中的模糊性，同时以高保真度推断全局状态。我们证明了GlobeDiff在单峰和多模态分布下的估计误差都有界。大量实验结果表明，GlobeDiff取得了卓越的性能，并且能够准确地推断全局状态。

title: DressWild: Feed-Forward Pose-Agnostic Garment Sewing Pattern Generation from In-the-Wild Images
summary: Recent advances in garment pattern generation have shown promising progress. However, existing feed-forward methods struggle with diverse poses and viewpoints, while optimization-based approaches are computationally expensive and difficult to scale. This paper focuses on sewing pattern generation for garment modeling and fabrication applications that demand editable, separable, and simulation-ready garments. We propose DressWild, a novel feed-forward pipeline that reconstructs physics-consistent 2D sewing patterns and the corresponding 3D garments from a single in-the-wild image. Given an input image, our method leverages vision-language models (VLMs) to normalize pose variations at the image level, then extract pose-aware, 3D-informed garment features. These features are fused through a transformer-based encoder and subsequently used to predict sewing pattern parameters, which can be directly applied to physical simulation, texture synthesis, and multi-layer virtual try-on. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach robustly recovers diverse sewing patterns and the corresponding 3D garments from in-the-wild images without requiring multi-view inputs or iterative optimization, offering an efficient and scalable solution for realistic garment simulation and animation.
tldr: The paper introduces DressWild, a feed-forward pipeline that uses vision-language models to generate 2D sewing patterns and 3D garments from single in-the-wild images, addressing the limitations of existing methods in handling diverse poses and computational efficiency.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DressWild，一个前馈管道，它使用视觉-语言模型从单张野外图像生成2D缝纫图案和3D服装，解决了现有方法在处理不同姿势和计算效率方面的局限性。
summary_zh: 服装纸样生成领域的最新进展展现了令人鼓舞的成果。然而，现有的前馈方法难以应对多样化的姿势和视角，而基于优化的方法则计算成本高昂且难以扩展。本文专注于为服装建模和制造应用生成缝纫纸样，这些应用需要可编辑、可分离且可用于仿真的服装。我们提出了一种新颖的前馈流程DressWild，它从单张自然场景图像中重建物理一致的2D缝纫纸样和相应的3D服装。给定输入图像，我们的方法利用视觉-语言模型（VLMs）来归一化图像层面的姿势变化，然后提取姿势感知的、3D信息驱动的服装特征。这些特征通过基于Transformer的编码器进行融合，并随后用于预测缝纫纸样参数，这些参数可以直接应用于物理仿真、纹理合成和多层虚拟试穿。大量的实验表明，我们的方法能够稳健地从自然场景图像中恢复多样化的缝纫纸样和相应的3D服装，而无需多视角输入或迭代优化，为逼真的服装仿真和动画提供了一种高效且可扩展的解决方案。

title: SAM 3D Body: Robust Full-Body Human Mesh Recovery
summary: We introduce SAM 3D Body (3DB), a promptable model for single-image full-body 3D human mesh recovery (HMR) that demonstrates state-of-the-art performance, with strong generalization and consistent accuracy in diverse in-the-wild conditions. 3DB estimates the human pose of the body, feet, and hands. It is the first model to use a new parametric mesh representation, Momentum Human Rig (MHR), which decouples skeletal structure and surface shape. 3DB employs an encoder-decoder architecture and supports auxiliary prompts, including 2D keypoints and masks, enabling user-guided inference similar to the SAM family of models. We derive high-quality annotations from a multi-stage annotation pipeline that uses various combinations of manual keypoint annotation, differentiable optimization, multi-view geometry, and dense keypoint detection. Our data engine efficiently selects and processes data to ensure data diversity, collecting unusual poses and rare imaging conditions. We present a new evaluation dataset organized by pose and appearance categories, enabling nuanced analysis of model behavior. Our experiments demonstrate superior generalization and substantial improvements over prior methods in both qualitative user preference studies and traditional quantitative analysis. Both 3DB and MHR are open-source.
tldr: The paper introduces SAM 3D Body (3DB), a promptable model for full-body 3D human mesh recovery with state-of-the-art performance, strong generalization, and a new parametric mesh representation (MHR), and presents a new evaluation dataset for detailed analysis.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 SAM 3D Body (3DB)，一个用于全身3D人体网格重建的可提示模型，拥有最先进的性能、强大的泛化能力和一种新的参数化网格表示(MHR)，并提出了一个新的评估数据集用于详细分析。
summary_zh: 我们提出了SAM 3D Body (3DB)，一个可提示的单图像全身3D人体网格重建（HMR）模型，在各种复杂的真实场景下表现出最先进的性能，具有强大的泛化能力和一致的精度。3DB估计人体、脚和手的姿态。它是第一个使用新型参数化网格表示——动量人体骨架（MHR）——的模型，该表示解耦了骨骼结构和表面形状。3DB采用编码器-解码器架构，并支持辅助提示，包括2D关键点和掩码，从而实现类似于SAM系列模型的用户引导推理。我们通过多阶段标注流程推导高质量的标注数据，该流程使用人工关键点标注、可微优化、多视图几何和密集关键点检测的各种组合。我们的数据引擎高效地选择和处理数据，以确保数据的多样性，收集不寻常的姿势和罕见的成像条件。我们提出了一个按姿势和外观类别组织的新评估数据集，能够对模型行为进行细致的分析。我们的实验证明了优越的泛化能力，并在定性用户偏好研究和传统的定量分析中，相对于先前方法有显著改进。3DB和MHR都是开源的。

title: A Scalable Approach to Solving Simulation-Based Network Security Games
summary: We introduce MetaDOAR, a lightweight meta-controller that augments the Double Oracle / PSRO paradigm with a learned, partition-aware filtering layer and Q-value caching to enable scalable multi-agent reinforcement learning on very large cyber-network environments. MetaDOAR learns a compact state projection from per node structural embeddings to rapidly score and select a small subset of devices (a top-k partition) on which a conventional low-level actor performs focused beam search utilizing a critic agent. Selected candidate actions are evaluated with batched critic forwards and stored in an LRU cache keyed by a quantized state projection and local action identifiers, dramatically reducing redundant critic computation while preserving decision quality via conservative k-hop cache invalidation. Empirically, MetaDOAR attains higher player payoffs than SOTA baselines on large network topologies, without significant scaling issues in terms of memory usage or training time. This contribution provide a practical, theoretically motivated path to efficient hierarchical policy learning for large-scale networked decision problems.
tldr: MetaDOAR, a meta-controller leveraging learned partitioning and Q-value caching, enables scalable multi-agent reinforcement learning for network security games on large cyber-networks, achieving superior performance without scaling issues.
tldr_zh: MetaDOAR是一种基于学习的分区和Q值缓存的元控制器，它实现了在大型网络安全游戏中可扩展的多智能体强化学习，并在没有扩展问题的情况下实现了卓越的性能。
summary_zh: 我们引入 MetaDOAR，一种轻量级的元控制器，它通过学习到的、分区感知的过滤层和 Q 值缓存来增强双 Oracle / PSRO 范式，从而实现对超大规模网络环境的可扩展多智能体强化学习。MetaDOAR 从每个节点的结构嵌入中学习到一个紧凑的状态投影，以快速评分并选择一小部分设备（一个 top-k 分区），一个常规的底层 actor 在其上执行聚焦波束搜索，并利用一个评论家智能体。选定的候选动作通过批量评论家前向传播进行评估，并存储在 LRU 缓存中，该缓存通过量化的状态投影和本地动作标识符进行索引，从而显著减少了冗余的评论计算，同时通过保守的 k 跳缓存失效保持了决策质量。在经验上，MetaDOAR 在大型网络拓扑结构上获得了比 SOTA 基线更高的玩家收益，并且在内存使用或训练时间方面没有明显的扩展问题。该贡献为大规模网络决策问题的高效分层策略学习提供了一条实用的、理论驱动的路径。

title: Vulnerability Analysis of Safe Reinforcement Learning via Inverse Constrained Reinforcement Learning
summary: Safe reinforcement learning (Safe RL) aims to ensure policy performance while satisfying safety constraints.
